frontier: Improve the direction review surface, the highest-stakes decision point in the loop
goal: Make the direction review moment legible and deliberate by showing what documents will change and providing clear context before approval

tasks.description: Add a header and sectioned layout to DirectionView orienting the user to what they are approving
tasks.instructions:
Rewrite src/modules/main-panel/direction.view.tsx to add a clear header and sectioned structure:

1. Header section:
   - 'Direction Proposal' title in --foreground
   - Subtitle in --foreground-muted: 'This updates your project direction and conventions'

2. Rationale section: keep the existing rationale content but give it a clear labeled container instead of a raw pre-block.

3. Keep all existing logic (approveDirection, discardDirection, acting, error states) and the Approve/Discard buttons at the bottom, restyled for consistency.

Leave document previews for a following task — this task only establishes the header and rationale structure.

tasks.description: Create a DocumentPreview component and wire the proposal's documents into DirectionView with view-full navigation
tasks.instructions:
Create src/components/document-preview.tsx and wire it into DirectionView in one coherent change:

1. DocumentPreview component, props: name (string), preview (string), onViewFull (callback). Render a labeled header (name in --foreground), a --code-bg preview area in monospace, and a 'view full document' affordance.

2. In direction.view.tsx, for each doc in direction.docs, derive a 5-7 line preview, render a DocumentPreview, and wire onViewFull to selectView({ kind: 'doc', name: doc.name }) via useMagent(). Always show direction.md and conventions.md; list additional docs below.

The component and its single consumer are built together since the component shape is driven by this exact use.
